title: Reading the Sea: Interdisciplinary Approaches to Coastal Studies
outline: Drawing on South Africa's rich history of marine social science, this course introduces students to the study of the ocean from an interdisciplinary perspective. Students are familiarised with the main physical and biological features of South Africa's coastline, and investigate the diversity of ways in which coastal relations, livelihoods, communities and industries are operated, maintained and regulated through policy, practice and protest. Each week, students are required to read two readings, with a designated rapporteur leading class discussions.
dp_requirements: 80% attendance for class sessions. Final paper submitted, rapporteur session completed, and 2 out of 3 reflection pieces submitted.
assessment: Reading journal 15%; class discussion 10%; reflection piece 15%; final essay 20%; exam 40%.
